2605.02447.title: PC-MNet: Dual-Level Congruity Modeling for Multimodal Sarcasm Detection via Polarity-Modulated Attention
2605.02447.paper_summary: Multimodal sarcasm detection, which aims to precisely identify pragmatic incongruities between literal text and nonverbal cues, has gained substantial attention in multimodal understanding. Recent advancements have predominantly relied on naïve similarity-based attention mechanisms and uniform late fusion strategies.Furthermore, given that functional entanglement restricts traditional late fusions, we incorporate a scalar congruity routing mechanism and a prior-guided contextual graph. This mechanism anchors a generalized incongruity manifold through a two-stage asymmetric optimization driven by inconsistency-aware contrastive learning, selectively fusing only the most discriminative multi-granularity evidence. Extensive experiments on the \texttt{MUStARD} benchmark and its spurious-correlation-mitigated balanced datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves new state-of-the-art performance, surpassing the strongest multimodal baseline by a substantial 3.14\% improvement in Macro-F1. By architecturally isolating atomic, composition, and contextual conflicts. This work provides a robust, decoupled paradigm for modeling subtle pragmatic incongruities in human communication.
2605.02447.paper_summary_zh: 多模態諷刺檢測旨在精確識別字面文本與非語言線索之間的實用不一致性，已在多模態理解中獲得了相當大的關注。最近的進展主要依賴於天真的相似性基礎注意機制和均勻的晚期融合策略。此外，由於功能糾纏限制了傳統的晚期融合，我們引入了一個標量一致性路由機制和一個先驗引導的上下文圖。該機制通過一個由不一致感知對比學習驅動的兩階段非對稱優化來錨定一個廣義的不一致性流形，選擇性地融合僅最具區分性的多粒度證據。在\texttt{MUStARD}基準及其減少虛假相關的平衡數據集上進行的廣泛實驗表明，我們的方法達到了新的最先進性能，超過最強多模態基線，Macro-F1指標提高了3.14\%。通過在架構上隔離原子、組合和上下文衝突，這項工作為建模人類交流中的微妙實用不一致性提供了一個穩健的解耦範式。
2605.02447.author: Maoheng Li et.al.
2605.02447.authors: Maoheng Li, Ling Zhou, Xiaohua Huang, Rubing Huang, Wenming Zheng, Guoying Zhao
